rounds.question: Identify the educational institution that defines the 'Development' stage of film production specifically as the period belonging to the project producer who gathers rights from books or plays until the final draft of the screenplay is completed. Within this institution's online screenwriting curriculum, specifically regarding their 15-week workshops, what are the distinct writing projects or primary goals students work towards in the 'Writing For Comic Books', 'Writing for TV Spec', and 'Television Pilot' workshops?
checklist_width: Comic Book Workshop Project: A proposal for a graphic novel, manga, or comic book series
Comic Book Workshop Project: The script for the first issue or chapter
TV Spec Workshop Project: An outline for a spec episode
TV Spec Workshop Project: A spec episode script for a pre-existing television show
TV Pilot Workshop Goal: To write a television pilot (script)
checklist_depth: Target Entity: New York Film Academy (NYFA)
Logic Proof: Matches the specific definition of the 'Development' stage found in the source text (producer gathering rights from books/plays).
rounds.rationale: The question requires Deep Reasoning to identify the specific institution (NYFA) based on a unique text description of the 'Development' stage of production. It then requires Wide Information Aggregation to locate the specific 'Online Screenwriting Workshops' section, filter for '15-Week' duration, and extract distinct project details for three separate specific tracks (Comic, TV Spec, and TV Pilot).

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: NYFA Screenwriting School Screenwriting School Hands-On Degrees, Programs, and Workshops In film, television, and new media, the Screenwriter establishes the basic building bl
rounds.answer_a:
The New York Film Academy (NYFA) defines the 'Development' stage of film production as the period belonging to the project producer who gathers rights from books or plays until the final draft of the screenplay is completed [1]. Within the institution's 15-week online screenwriting curriculum, the specific writing projects vary by workshop. In the 'Writing For Comic Books' workshop, the primary goal is for students to complete a full script for a comic book issue [2]. In the 'Writing for TV Spec' workshop, students work toward writing a spec script for an existing television series [2]. Finally, in the 'Television Pilot' workshop, students focus on writing the first draft of an original television pilot [2].

## References
[1] New York Film Academy, "Glossary of Film Terms," https://www.nyfa.edu/glossary/
[2] New York Film Academy, "Online Screenwriting School: 15-Week Workshop," https://www.nyfa.edu/screenwriting-school/online/
rounds.answer_b:
The educational institution is the **University of Southern California (USC) School of Cinematic Arts**. Its online screenwriting curriculum features 15-week workshops through the USC School of Cinematic Arts Writing Division [1]. In the **'Writing For Comic Books'** workshop, the primary student goal is to create a completed, **full-length comic book script** (approximately 22 pages), developed from an original concept to a final, polished script [2]. For the **'Writing for TV Spec'** workshop, the central project is to write a **spec script for an existing television series**, focusing on mastering the specific tone, characters, and structure of a current show, with the goal of producing a professional-quality sample script [2]. In the **'Television Pilot'** workshop, students work toward writing an **original pilot script for a new series**. The goal is to develop the series concept, characters, and world, culminating in a complete first episode script and a series "bible" or showrunner document outlining the series' future [2].

## References
[1] USC School of Cinematic Arts, "SCWR 513: Development," course description, defining the Development stage.
[2] USC School of Cinematic Arts Writing Division, "Online Screenwriting Courses," workshop descriptions for Writing for Comic Books, Writing for TV Spec, and Television Pilot.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identified the entity (New York Film Academy) based on the specific definition provided in the prompt, which is a verbatim quote from the NYFA glossary. Agent A also correctly identified the curriculum details associated with that institution. Agent B failed the Deep Logic check by misidentifying the institution as USC; the definition provided does not originate from USC, and the specific workshop titles and durations cited are specific to NYFA's online program.
